description: Comprehensive mapping review policy based on original mapping prompt guidelines. Deterministic mapping remains source of truth; this policy governs structured override suggestions.

balance_sheet.name: Tangible and acquired intangible assets - opening/closing balance
Shares and shareholdings - General balance sheet item
Change in value and write-downs on capital assets - General balance sheet item
Accrued special payroll tax on pension

income_statement.name: Non-tax sensitive - Profit and loss statement
Change in value and write-downs on capital assets
Sponsorship, donations and gifts - presumed deductible
Sponsorship, donations and gifts - presumed non-deductible
Entertainment - internal and external - presumed deductible
Entertainment - internal and external - presumed non-deductible
Warranty provision - Change in warranty provision
Pension costs and basis for special payroll tax on pension cost
Special payroll tax on pension cost
Capital assets (Shares) - Unrealized change in value
Interest income on the tax account
Interest cost on the tax account
Tax allocation reserve - this year's change

decisionHierarchy.instruction: Use policy-pack rules and deterministic evidence first.
Specific guideline rules override generic tax intuition.
If uncertainty remains, choose prudent treatment and set review flag.
Never propose categories outside the allowed catalog.
Fallback to non-tax-sensitive category by statement type when no specific category is reliable.

guidelineRules.instruction: Balances for capitalized expenditure (balanserad utgift/aktiverad utgift) and associated depreciation are generally non-tax-sensitive, unless evidence indicates building or similar specific acquisition category.

Balance sheet accounts for building, land improvement, and leasehold accumulated depreciation should map to non-tax-sensitive balance category; these depreciation categories are recognized in income statement mapping.
Land (mark) is non-depreciable and should be treated as non-tax-sensitive in balance sheet mapping.
Generic accumulated depreciation (ackumulerad avskrivning) should map to Tangible and acquired intangible assets - opening/closing balance (102000), not accelerated depreciation categories.
WIP or on-going projects (pågående projekt/pågående arbete) are generally non-tax-sensitive in balance sheet mapping.
Accrual/provision signals (upplupen/accrued/provision/reservering/avsättning) generally map to Other provisions (229000), unless a more specific category applies.
Accrued property tax/property fee liabilities should map to Property tax and property fee (251300) rather than generic provisions.
Group contribution receivables should not be treated as generic provisions.

Intra-group receivables may map to Change in value and write-downs on capital assets - General balance sheet item (138400) when closing balance is lower than opening balance, indicating potential impairment.

Estimated/accrued special payroll tax on pension (for example löneskatt innevarande år with accrual/provision context) should generally map to 294300, but not every special payroll tax liability qualifies.
Balance sheet accounts must not map to Result of the year (940000).

Bad debt/doubtful receivable signals (osäkra kundfordringar, tvistiga kundfordringar, dubious receivables, revaluation of receivables) support Doubtful debts (151500). Generic receivables should not map to doubtful debts.
Leasing cost accounts should generally map to Interest - financial leasing - cost (522200).
Booked depreciation for generic tangible/intangible assets (excluding building and land improvement specific cases) should map to 397000.
Building booked depreciation maps to 777000 and land improvement booked depreciation maps to 782400.
Differentiate building maintenance/repair from building depreciation. Maintenance should generally be non-tax-sensitive (950000).
COGS and related sold goods cost accounts (including wording about cost-of-goods-sold depreciation) are non-tax-sensitive and should not be treated as depreciation categories.
Tax itself maps to Special payroll tax on pension cost (753000). Pension costs forming tax base map to 740000. Accrued special payroll tax category (294300) is balance-sheet only.

Consulting/legal fees connected to income tax assistance (for example INK2 prep) map to Consulting fees (655000). IT/software/hosting/support consulting should generally be non-tax-sensitive (950000). When tax assistance may be hidden in broader labels such as accounting fees, apply prudent review-oriented judgment.
Partially deductible representation should be treated prudently as non-deductible entertainment (607200).
Social contributions/arbetsgivaravgifter generally map to non-tax-sensitive (950000) and must be differentiated from special payroll tax items (753000).
Generic salary/employment costs should generally remain non-tax-sensitive (950000), not pension base category (740000), unless pension-specific evidence exists.
Interest income on tax account maps to 831400; interest cost on tax account maps to 842300. Signals can include tax exempt/non-deductible wording.
Banking costs (for example bankkostnader) should generally map to Interest - Banking costs (657000) even if account numbering is atypical.
FX gains/losses should generally map to 843100/843600 even when account numbers appear operating rather than financial.
Membership fees are usually non-deductible (698200). Payments to employers' associations for conflict purposes should map to deductible membership fees (698100).
Entertainment is generally non-deductible (607200). Staff catering, employee travel meals, and staff events are generally non-tax-sensitive unless specific representation signals indicate otherwise.
Gifts are generally non-deductible (699300). Small staff gifts (for example flowers and some Christmas gifts) may map to deductible gifts category (598000).
